calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Vacated

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lauren Veggian, Esq. 619-483-1198 of the Cardoza Law Corporation for Plaintiff through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GRAHAM NELSON'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES.

Plaintiff Graham Nelson's motion for attorneys' fees and costs is granted in the amount of $11,575.49 in attorneys' fees and $750.45 in costs.

The Court finds that the amount of the award plaintiff is excessive for the quality and nature of the work performed, and that not all of the time spent by plaintiff's counsel was "necessarily expended" to enforce defendant's liability under the Rosenthal Fair Debt Collection Practices Act. (Civ. Code 1788.30(c).) This finding is based on the Court's review of the record and of counsel's time records, taking into account among other things counsel's expertise in the subject matter, defendants' early settlement offers, and the fact that one attorney billed (albeit at a lower hourly rate) for clerical tasks such as filing and service. The Court will not award any fees for travel to or attendance at the hearing on the fees motion. The Court finds that calculation of a reasonable lodestar requires a 25% reduction of the remaining hours for which compensation is sought (46 hours), to 34.5 hours. The Court further finds that plaintiff has made a sufficient showing as to the reasonable hourly rates for counsel's time, and that defendants have not rebutted that showing. The amount resulting from these adjustments, which the Court finds to be the reasonable lodestar, is $11,575.49.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-21-2019 Set For Trial On Apr-27-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
